hero.title: Bank Access for AI-natives
hero.subtitle: Connect your European Bank to Claude, ChatGPT or OpenClaw with custom skills, MCP and API. Let AI handle your budgeting, invoice matching and bookkeeping.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your use of the FiBuKI application, operated by Infinity Vertigo GmbH.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By registering for or using FiBuKI, you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the application.

serviceDescription.title: 2. Service Description
serviceDescription.content:
FiBuKI is software designed to assist with bookkeeping pre-accounting. The application provides:

• Management of bank transactions
• Automatic receipt matching
• AI-powered categorization
• Integration with bank accounts and email

The application does not replace professional tax or accounting advice.

useAtOwnRisk.title: 3. Use at Your Own Risk
useAtOwnRisk.content:
Use of FiBuKI is at your own risk. You are responsible for the accuracy of your inputs and the proper maintenance of your accounting records.

We strongly recommend reviewing all automatically generated categorizations and matches before using them for tax purposes.

liability.title: 4. Limitation of Liability
liability.content:
Infinity Vertigo GmbH is not liable for:

• Damages resulting from incorrect AI categorizations or matches
• Financial losses from using the application
• Tax disadvantages due to errors in the application
• Data loss outside our control
• Service outages or interruptions

Liability is limited to cases of intent and gross negligence. Liability for lost profits or indirect damages is excluded.

noIndemnification.title: 5. No Indemnification
noIndemnification.content: Users have no claim to indemnification from third-party claims arising from use of the application. Users are solely responsible for compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

fees.title: 6. Fees and Payments
fees.content:
Fees may apply for using FiBuKI. Current pricing and subscription options are communicated within the application.

• Fee changes will be announced at least 30 days in advance
• Fees already paid are non-refundable
• Access may be restricted for payment defaults

accountTermination.title: 7. Account Termination
accountTermination.content:
We reserve the right to terminate or suspend user accounts at any time without stating reasons, particularly in cases of:

• Violation of these Terms
• Misuse of the application
• Fraudulent activities
• Non-payment of due fees

You may delete your account at any time in the settings.

intellectualProperty.title: 8. Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.content: All rights to the FiBuKI software, including trademarks, logos, and source code, remain with Infinity Vertigo GmbH. The usage license is non-transferable and non-exclusive.

dataHandling.title: 9. Data Processing
dataHandling.content: Processing of your personal data is governed by our Privacy Policy. By using the application, you consent to the data processing described therein.

changes.title: 10. Changes to Terms
changes.content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be announced via email or within the application. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.

jurisdiction.title: 11. Governing Law
jurisdiction.content: Austrian law applies, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. The place of jurisdiction is Wiener Neustadt, Austria.

contact.title: 12. Contact
contact.content:
For questions about these Terms:

Infinity Vertigo GmbH
Bergwald 43
2812 Hollenthon
Austria

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal data when using FiBuKI.

responsible.title: Data Controller
responsible.content:
Infinity Vertigo GmbH
Bergwald 43
2812 Hollenthon
Austria

Commercial Register: FN571837m
VAT ID: ATU77919424

dataCollection.title: Data We Collect
dataCollection.content:
We collect and process the following categories of personal data:

• Account data: Name, email address for authentication
• Financial data: Bank transactions, receipts, invoices you upload or connect
• Email data: Gmail messages and attachments (only with your explicit consent)
• Usage data: Interactions with the application for improvements

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.content:
We process your personal data on the following legal bases under Art. 6 GDPR:

• Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)): Processing necessary for the core functionality of FiBuKI, including transaction management, receipt matching, and AI-powered categorization
• Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)): Gmail integration is only activated with your explicit consent. You can revoke this consent at any time by disconnecting your Gmail account
• Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)): Usage analytics and AI quality monitoring to improve the service. You can object to this processing at any time

services.title: Third-Party Services
services.intro: We use the following third-party services to provide our services:

firebase.name: Firebase (Google)
firebase.purpose: Authentication, database, file storage, and cloud functions. Your data is stored in the EU (europe-west1).

gmailApi.name: Gmail API (Google)
gmailApi.purpose: Email access for receipt search. Only active when you connect your Gmail account via OAuth. You can revoke access at any time.

cloudVision.name: Google Cloud Vision API
cloudVision.purpose: Text recognition (OCR) in uploaded documents for automatic data extraction.

vertexAi.name: Vertex AI / Gemini (Google)
vertexAi.purpose: AI-powered document analysis, categorization, and intelligent matching of receipts.

anthropic.name: Anthropic Claude API
anthropic.purpose: AI chat assistant for accounting questions and intelligent support.

truelayer.name: TrueLayer
truelayer.purpose: Open Banking access (UK/EU) for securely fetching your account transactions.

langfuse.name: LangFuse
langfuse.purpose: Analysis and optimization of AI interactions for quality improvement.

googleUserData.title: Google User Data
googleUserData.content: FiBuKI accesses Google user data through the Gmail API to help you find and match receipts and invoices to your bank transactions.

whatWeAccess.title: What We Access
whatWeAccess.content:
• Gmail messages: Subject lines, sender information, dates, and body content of emails that may contain receipts or invoices
• Gmail attachments: PDF, image, and document attachments for receipt extraction
• Google account profile: Name and email address for authentication

howWeUse.title: How We Use This Data
howWeUse.content:
• Receipt search: We scan your emails for receipts and invoices matching your bank transactions
• Data extraction: We extract amounts, dates, and vendor information from email content and attachments
• Matching: Extracted data is compared against your bank transactions for automatic matching

limitedUse.title: Limited Use Disclosure
limitedUse.content:
FiBuKI's use and transfer of information received from Google APIs adheres to the Google API Services User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements.

Specifically:
• We only use Google user data for the purposes described in this privacy policy
• We do not transfer Google user data to third parties, except as necessary to provide or improve the service, or as required by law
• We do not use Google user data for serving advertisements
• We do not allow humans to read Google user data, except with your affirmative consent, for security purposes, to comply with applicable law, or for internal operations where the data has been aggregated and anonymized

revocation.title: Revoking Access
revocation.content:
You can revoke FiBuKI's access to your Google data at any time:

• In FiBuKI: Go to Settings > Integrations and disconnect your Gmail account
• In Google: Visit your Google Account permissions at myaccount.google.com/permissions

When you revoke access, all stored email data and OAuth tokens are deleted within 24 hours.

internationalTransfers.title: International Data Transfers
internationalTransfers.content:
Your data is primarily stored within the European Union (Google Cloud, region europe-west1). However, some processing involves transfers to the United States:

• Anthropic (Claude AI): Your chat messages and accounting queries are processed by Anthropic's servers in the US. This transfer is based on standard contractual clauses (SCCs) pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR
• Google Cloud AI (Vertex AI, Cloud Vision): Document analysis may be processed in Google's global infrastructure, covered by Google's Data Processing Amendment and SCCs

All international data transfers are protected by appropriate safeguards as required by Chapter V of the GDPR.

dataProtection.title: Data Protection Mechanisms
dataProtection.content:
We implement comprehensive security measures to protect your data:

• Encryption in transit: All data transmitted between your device and our servers is encrypted using TLS 1.3
• Encryption at rest: All data stored in our databases (Google Cloud Firestore) and file storage (Google Cloud Storage) is encrypted using AES-256
• Access controls: Your data is only accessible to you via your authenticated account. Our team does not access your personal data except for technical support when explicitly requested by you
• Infrastructure security: Our services run on Google Cloud Platform, which maintains SOC 2, ISO 27001, and other security certifications

emailData.title: Email Data (Gmail Integration)
emailData.content:
When you connect your Gmail account:

• We access only email metadata and attachments relevant to receipts and invoices
• Email content is processed to extract transaction-relevant information (amounts, dates, vendors)
• We do not store full email content; only extracted data is retained
• You can disconnect your Gmail account and delete all associated data at any time

retention.title: Data Retention & Deletion
retention.content:
• Your data is retained while your account is active
• You can delete individual files, transactions, or connected accounts at any time
• You can delete your account via Settings > Sign-in & Security > Delete Account
• Account deletion includes a 30-day grace period during which you can cancel the request
• After the grace period, all associated data is permanently removed
• OAuth tokens for connected services (e.g., Gmail) are revoked and cannot be recovered
• Database backups are retained for up to 90 days for disaster recovery, after which they are automatically purged

automatedProcessing.title: Automated Processing
automatedProcessing.content:
FiBuKI uses automated processing, including AI and machine learning, for the following purposes:

• Receipt matching: Automatically matching uploaded receipts to bank transactions based on amounts, dates, and vendor information
• Categorization: AI-powered categorization of transactions and receipts
• Partner detection: Automatic identification of business partners from transaction descriptions and documents
• Scoring: Confidence scores for suggested matches between files and transactions

These automated processes are designed to assist you and do not produce decisions with legal or similarly significant effects. All suggested matches and categorizations can be reviewed, corrected, or rejected by you at any time.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.content:
Under GDPR, you have the following rights:

• Right of access (Art. 15): You can request information about your stored data
• Rectification (Art. 16): You can have incorrect data corrected
• Erasure (Art. 17): You can request deletion of your data
• Restriction (Art. 18): You can request restriction of processing
• Data portability (Art. 20): You can receive your data in a common format
• Objection (Art. 21): You can object to processing
• Automated decisions (Art. 22): You have the right not to be subject to purely automated decisions with legal effect

To exercise your rights, contact us at privacy@fibuki.com.

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Austrian Data Protection Authority:
Österreichische Datenschutzbehörde
Barichgasse 40–42
1030 Vienna, Austria
https://dsb.gv.at

contact.title: Contact
contact.content:

liability.content: The contents of this website have been created with the utmost care. However, we cannot guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the content. As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages in accordance with general laws. However, we are not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances that indicate illegal activity.

copyright.title: Copyright
